data.title: Earnings Flash (WISE.L) Wise Reports FY25 Revenue GBP1.21B
HSBC Buys Back Shares
data.description:
HSBC repurchased 2,481,921 shares on Wednesday, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The lender repurchased 1,452,321 shares in the UK at a volume-weighted average price of 8.7378 pounds sterling apiece and 1,029,600 shares in Hong Kong for HK$92.7645 per share.

HSBC has so far repurchased 132,191,751 shares for nearly $1.54 billion since the current buyback program began May 6.

Following the cancellation of shares repurchased in the UK, the firm has 17,559,642,786 ordinary shares in issue.

data.title: REG - CMC Markets Plc - Board and Directorate Changes
data.description:
RNS Number : 5494L CMC Markets Plc 05 June 2025  

05 June 2025

CMC Markets plc ("CMC") 

Board and Directorate Changes

CMC Markets plc (LON:CMCX) today announces several changes to its Board of Directors and senior leadership team.

·      David Fineberg, currently Deputy Chief Executive Officer and Board Director, will not stand for re-election at the 2025 Annual General Meeting ("AGM"). He will transition into the newly created role of Global Head of Strategic Partnerships, with a focus on strengthening key institutional relationships and accelerating growth through partnerships such as Revolut and StrikeX.

·      Matthew Lewis, currently Head of ANZ and Board Director, will also step down from the Board at the 2025 AGM. He will focus fully on expanding the Group's footprint across the ANZ region, particularly in stockbroking and digital asset services.

·      The Board is pleased to confirm the appointment of Laurence Booth to the Board as an Executive Director, effective 5 June 2025. Laurence will continue in his role as Global Head of Capital Markets, bringing extensive global execution and capital markets expertise to the Board.1

·      As previously announced, James Richards, Non-Executive Chairman, will step down at the 2025 AGM. James has served on the Board since 2015 and as Chairman since 2018. The Board is pleased to confirm that Paul Wainscott, currently Senior Independent Director, will succeed James as Chairman following the AGM.

The Board would like to express its sincere thanks to David Fineberg and Matthew Lewis for their Board service and looks forward to their continued contributions in their respective leadership roles. The Board also extends its deep gratitude to James Richards for his dedication and leadership over the past decade.

1 There is no other information to be disclosed under UK Listing Rule 6.4.8R (1) to (6) in relation to Laurence Booth.

END

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Sanhua Intelligent Controls Passes Hong Kong Listing Hearing; Shares Rise 3%
data.description:
Zhejiang Sanhua Intelligent Controls published the post-hearing version of its preliminary prospectus, signaling that the company passed the listing hearing.

The maker of refrigeration and air-conditioning control components filed its initial public offering application with the Hong Kong Exchange in January, according to a Shenzhen bourse disclosure on Thursday.

The stock jumped over 3% in recent trade.

data.title: South Korean Nuclear Energy Stocks Surge on Czech Deal
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun

South Korean nuclear energy stocks surged after the Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power-led consortium finalized an estimated $19 billion deal in the Czech Republic.

KHNP, an affiliate of state utility Korea Electric Power, said Thursday that it had signed a contract with Czech state power company, Elektrarna Dukovany II, to build two new nuclear reactors for the Dukovany power plant, southeast of Prague.

Shares of KEPCO Engineering & Construction, a KHNP subsidiary responsible for designing the new nuclear power units, jumped as much as 24% to 75,700 won in early afternoon trading, outperforming the benchmark Kospi's 1.1% gain. Another KHNP consortium member Doosan Enerbility, a nuclear reactor supplier, gained 8.4% to 46,250 won.

The Dukovany contract is valued at around 26 trillion won, equivalent to $19.09 billion, KHNP said.

Signing of the contract--originally due in May--had been delayed by a Czech lower court's injunction following a legal complaint filed by a defeated French bidder. However, an appeals court on Wednesday dismissed the lower court's decision and gave the green light to the KHNP consortium, according to KHNP.

The contract marks South Korea's second major nuclear energy project overseas after it built a Barakah nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates, KHNP said.

The KHNP consortium won the bid in the Czech Republic last year, beating rival France's EDF. The Central European country is seeking to expand its nuclear energy power grid to wean itself off fossil fuels and achieve carbon neutrality.

Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com

About Diversified Energy Company PLC

Diversified is a leading publicly traded energy company focused on natural gas and liquids production, transport, marketing, and well retirement. Through our differentiated strategy, we acquire existing, long-life assets and invest in them to improve environmental and operational performance until retiring those assets in a safe and environmentally secure manner. Recognized by ratings agencies and organizations for our sustainability leadership, this solutions-oriented, stewardship approach makes Diversified the Right Company at the Right Time to responsibly produce energy, deliver reliable free cash flow, and generate shareholder value.

data.title: Earnings Flash (GFM.L) Griffin Mining Reports FY24 EPS $0.06
Poland - Factors to Watch June 5
data.description:
The following are significant news stories, press reports and events on Thursday. ALL TIMES GMT (Poland: GMT + 2 hours):

INTEREST RATES

Poland's central bank left its main interest rate unchanged on Wednesday as expected, and assessed that the current level is conducive to achieving its inflation target.

On Thursday at 1300 there will be a conference by the Governor of the National Bank of Poland (NBP), Adam Glapinski, who will present the justification for Wednesday's decision of the Monetary Policy Council (MPC).

RULING COALITION MEETING

The leaders of the Civic Coalition, Third Way and New Left are to discuss further plans for cooperation within the Polish government coalition, a possible reshuffle of the cabinet and potential changes to the coalition agreement on Thursday, PAP news agency reported.

The Romanian leu is set to languish near record lows against the euro the rest of the year, a Reuters poll showed on Wednesday, with no recovery in sight after a closely contested presidential election highlighted the fiscal and economic challenges ahead.

DEBT

On Thursday at 1300, the Polish Ministry of Finance announced the supply value for the Treasury bill auction scheduled for Friday.

BUDIMEX

The CEO of Budimex , Poland's largest construction company, said that the reconstruction of Ukraine will become one of the largest infrastructure projects in Europe and the company intends to get involved, Dziennik Gazeta Prawna daily reported.

****Reuters has not verified stories reported by Polish media and does not vouch for their accuracy.****

data.title: EPL to Invest Additional 43 Million Baht in Thailand-Based Wholly-Owned Subsidiary
data.description:
EPL has invested an additional 43 million Thai Baht in its Thailand-based wholly-owned subsidiary EPL Packaging (Thailand), according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The investment is subject to approvals as per the provisions of applicable laws in India and Thailand, and is expected to be completed by July 15.

EPL Packaging (Thailand) has been formed to pursue various growth opportunities in Thailand for the manufacturing and trading of laminated tubes.

The company's shares were up nearly 2% in recent trade.

data.title: European Markets Seen Flat Ahead of ECB Rate Decision
data.description:
European equity markets were poised for a muted open on Thursday as investors awaited the latest European Central Bank policy decision, with a widely expected 25 basis point rate cut on the table.

Expectations for a cut were reinforced by recent data showing Eurozone inflation eased to 1.9% in May, coming in below forecasts and falling just under the ECB’s 2% target, solidifying the case for easing.

Globally, sentiment remained fragile amid ongoing trade tensions, after US President Donald Trump said it was “extremely hard” to reach a deal with Chinese President Xi Jinping, despite earlier indications that talks between the two leaders could happen this week.

In premarket trade, Euro Stoxx 50 and Stoxx 600 futures hovered near the flatline, reflecting caution ahead of the ECB announcement and broader geopolitical uncertainty.

data.title: Shyndec Pharma Gets Registration Certificate for Hypertension Drug
data.description:
Shanghai Shyndec Pharmaceutical obtained a drug registration certificate for imidapril hydrochloride tablets from China's National Medical Products Administration, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Thursday.

The pharmaceutical company invested 7.7 million yuan into the research and development of the drug, which is used to treat hypertension, the filing said.

The company's shares slipped over 1% in recent trade.
